questions.question: Define the three types of notation for arithmetic expressions: infix, prefix, and postfix. Explain their differences with examples.
keyPoints: Definition of infix notation with example
Definition of prefix notation with example
Definition of postfix notation with example
Comparison of the three notations

questions.question: List the advantages of postfix notation over infix notation for computer evaluation.
keyPoints: No parentheses needed
No precedence rules required
Easy to evaluate using a stack
Efficient for compilers

questions.question: Explain the infix-to-postfix conversion algorithm using a stack. Trace the algorithm for the expression: A * (B + C) - D / E.
keyPoints: Complete algorithm with all rules for operands, operators, and parentheses
Operator precedence and associativity handling
Step-by-step trace table with columns: Symbol, Stack, Output
Handling of parentheses (push '(', pop until '(' for ')')
Final step: pop all remaining operators
Correct final postfix expression: A B C + * D E / -

questions.question: Describe the postfix evaluation algorithm using a stack and apply it to the expression: 6 2 3 + - 3 8 2 / + * 2 ^ 3 +.
keyPoints: Algorithm: scan left to right, push operands, pop two for operators
Correct operand order for non-commutative operators
Step-by-step trace table with columns: Symbol, Stack, Operation
Intermediate calculations shown clearly
Final result on the stack

questions.question: Write a C program to evaluate a postfix expression using a stack. The program should read a postfix expression as a string and compute the result.
keyPoints: Stack implementation for integer/float operands
Scanning the postfix string character by character
Converting character digits to integers
Handling all four arithmetic operators (+, -, *, /)
Correct operand order when popping (second_popped OP first_popped)
Displaying the final result
